# Sirr (سر) — Ephemeral Secret Vault

Sirr is a self-hosted ephemeral secret vault with multi-tenant support.
Single Rust binary, zero runtime dependencies, end-to-end encrypted.

## What It Does

- Store secrets with TTL (time-to-live) and/or read limits
- Secrets auto-delete when expired or after N reads
- Multi-tenant: organizations, principals, roles, and named API keys
- Bearer token authentication, ChaCha20Poly1305 encryption at rest
- File-based master key delivery for production (avoids env var exposure)
- Audit logging for all secret operations
- Patchable secrets (seal instead of burn, update value via PATCH)
- CLI, HTTP API, Node.js SDK, Python SDK, .NET SDK, MCP server

## Primary Use Cases

- Share database credentials with AI assistants that auto-expire (read-once)
- CI/CD tokens that burn after a single deploy
- Team secret management with role-based access control
- Contractor API access with automatic expiry
- Team .env sync with rolling TTL

## Authentication

Two auth modes:
- **Master key** (`SIRR_MASTER_API_KEY`): full access, used for bootstrapping and public bucket writes
- **Principal key** (Bearer token): org-scoped, role-based permissions, time-windowed

## HTTP API — Public Bucket (backward compatible)

Reads are unauthenticated. Writes require master key.

POST   /secrets          Store a secret: {key, value, ttl_seconds?, max_reads?, delete?}
GET    /secrets          List metadata (no values returned, requires master key)
GET    /secrets/:key     Retrieve + decrement read counter (404 if expired/burned)
HEAD   /secrets/:key     Check existence without consuming a read
PATCH  /secrets/:key     Update value (only if delete=false, requires master key)
DELETE /secrets/:key     Immediate delete (requires master key)
POST   /prune            Delete all expired secrets now
GET    /health           Health check (no auth required)

## HTTP API — Org-Scoped (multi-tenant)

All org routes require principal key auth (`Authorization: Bearer <key>`).

### Org management (master key only)
POST   /orgs                              Create org: {name}
GET    /orgs                              List orgs
DELETE /orgs/:org_id                      Delete org (must have no principals)

### Principal management (master key only)
POST   /orgs/:org_id/principals           Create principal: {name, role}
GET    /orgs/:org_id/principals           List principals
DELETE /orgs/:org_id/principals/:id       Delete principal (must have no keys)

### Role management (master key only)
POST   /orgs/:org_id/roles               Create custom role: {name, permissions}
GET    /orgs/:org_id/roles               List roles
DELETE /orgs/:org_id/roles/:name         Delete custom role

### Self-service (any principal)
GET    /me                                Current principal info + keys
PATCH  /me                                Update display name / metadata
POST   /me/keys                           Create named API key
DELETE /me/keys/:key_id                   Delete own key

### Org-scoped secrets (permission-based)
POST   /orgs/:org_id/secrets              Create secret (CreateSecret perm)
GET    /orgs/:org_id/secrets              List secrets (ListOrg or ListMy)
GET    /orgs/:org_id/secrets/:key         Read secret (ReadOrg or ReadMy)
HEAD   /orgs/:org_id/secrets/:key         Check secret (ReadOrg or ReadMy)
PATCH  /orgs/:org_id/secrets/:key         Update sealed secret (PatchOrg or PatchMy)
DELETE /orgs/:org_id/secrets/:key         Delete secret (DeleteOrg or DeleteMy)
POST   /orgs/:org_id/prune               Prune expired (Prune perm)

### Org audit & webhooks
GET    /orgs/:org_id/audit                Query audit log (AuditRead perm)
POST   /orgs/:org_id/webhooks            Register webhook (WebhookManage perm)
GET    /orgs/:org_id/webhooks            List webhooks (WebhookManage perm)
DELETE /orgs/:org_id/webhooks/:id        Delete webhook (WebhookManage perm)

## Built-in Roles

| Role   | Permissions         | Description                              |
|--------|---------------------|------------------------------------------|
| reader | rla                 | Read own secrets, list own, own account  |
| writer | rlcpdam             | CRUD own secrets + manage account        |
| admin  | rRlLcCpPaAmMdD      | Full org access, manage principals/roles |
| owner  | rRlLcCpPaAmMSdD     | Admin + SirrAdmin (manage orgs)          |

15 permission bits: r(ReadMy) R(ReadOrg) l(ListMy) L(ListOrg) c(CreateSecret) C(reserved)
p(PatchMy) P(PatchOrg) a(AccountSelf) A(AccountAdmin) m(DeleteMy) M(DeleteOrg)
d(Prune) D(AuditRead) S(SirrAdmin)

## CLI

sirrd serve                              Start server
sirrd serve --init                       Start + auto-create default org/admin/keys
sirr push KEY=value [--ttl 1h] [--reads 1] [--org ORG]
sirr push .env [--ttl 24h] [--org ORG]  Push all keys from .env file
sirr get KEY [--org ORG]                 Print value to stdout
sirr pull .env [--org ORG]               Write all secrets to .env file
sirr run [--org ORG] -- <cmd>            Exec command with secrets as env vars
sirr share KEY [--org ORG]               Print URL reference
sirr list [--org ORG]                    Show active secrets (metadata)
sirr delete KEY [--org ORG]
sirr prune [--org ORG]                   Delete all expired now
sirr orgs list|create|delete             Org management (master key)
sirr principals list|create|delete --org Manage principals (master key)
sirr me info|keys|create-key|delete-key  Self-service (principal key)
sirr audit [--org ORG]                   Query audit log
sirr webhooks list|add|remove [--org ORG]

## Environment Variables

SIRR_MASTER_API_KEY           Master key for server auth
SIRR_LICENSE_KEY       License key for tier limits (get at sirrlock.com/pricing)
SIRR_PORT              HTTP port (default: 39999)
SIRR_HOST              Bind address (default: 0.0.0.0)
SIRR_DATA_DIR          Storage dir (default: ~/.local/share/sirr/)
SIRR_SERVER            CLI: server URL (default: sirr://localhost:39999)
SIRR_TOKEN             CLI: bearer token
SIRR_ORG               CLI/MCP: org ID for multi-tenant mode
SIRR_ENABLE_PUBLIC_BUCKET   Set to false to disable legacy /secrets routes (default: true)
SIRR_AUTOINIT          Set to true to auto-create org/admin on first boot

## SDKs

### Node.js (@sirr/sdk)
import { SirrClient } from '@sirr/sdk'
const c = new SirrClient({ server: '...', token: '...', org: 'my-org' })
await c.push('KEY', 'value', { ttl: 3600, reads: 1 })
const v = await c.get('KEY')
await c.me()
await c.createOrg({ name: 'team' })

### Python (sirr)
from sirr import SirrClient
c = SirrClient(server='...', token='...', org='my-org')
c.push('KEY', 'value', ttl=3600, max_reads=1)
v = c.get('KEY')
c.me()
c.create_org(name='team')

### .NET (Sirr.Client)
var client = new SirrClient(new SirrOptions { Server = "...", Token = "...", Org = "my-org" });
await client.PushAsync("KEY", "value");
var v = await client.GetAsync("KEY");
await client.GetMeAsync();

## MCP Server (@sirrlock/mcp)

Configure in .mcp.json with SIRR_SERVER, SIRR_TOKEN, and optionally SIRR_ORG.
Tools: get_secret, push_secret, list_secrets, delete_secret, prune_secrets,
       sirr_me, sirr_create_key, sirr_delete_key

## Security Model

- Master key delivered via file or env var (SIRR_MASTER_API_KEY)
- ChaCha20Poly1305 encrypts each secret value with a random per-record nonce
- Encryption key is a random 32-byte file (sirr.key), generated on first boot
- Metadata (TTL, read count) stored plaintext for efficient expiry scanning
- Constant-time bearer token comparison prevents timing attacks
- Org isolation: principals can only access secrets in their own org
- Ownership enforcement: *My permissions restrict access to own secrets only
- Key binding: secrets can restrict which named keys can read them (allowed_keys)
- Audit logging on all CRUD operations
- No telemetry, no phone-home

## Storage

- sirr.db   — redb embedded key-value database (single file)
- sirr.key  — 32-byte encryption key, generated on first run

## Licensing

BSL 1.1: source-available, converts to Apache 2.0 on February 20, 2028.

| Tier        | Orgs | Principals | Price      |
|-------------|------|------------|------------|
| Solo (free) | 1    | 1          | $0         |
| Solo+       | 1    | 5          | $199/year  |
| Team        | 1    | unlimited  | $499/year  |
| Business    | unlimited | unlimited | $1,999/year |

License keys at: https://sirrlock.com/pricing

## Repository Layout

crates/sirr-server/   Rust library: axum server, redb store, crypto, auth, org handlers
crates/sirr/          Rust binary: CLI client (clap + reqwest)
crates/sirrd/         Rust binary: server daemon
Dockerfile            FROM scratch + musl binary
docker-compose.yml    Production setup with key file mount
